questions.q: What is the general formula for alkanes?
questions.explanation: Alkanes are saturated hydrocarbons with the general formula CnH2n+2. This is because each carbon atom has four valence electrons and forms bonds with four other atoms, either carbon or hydrogen.

questions.q: Which type of isomerism is exhibited by butane?
questions.explanation: Butane exhibits structural isomerism due to the presence of two different structures: n-butane and isobutane.

questions.q: What is the process of removing a hydrogen atom from an alkane called?
questions.explanation: Dehydrogenation is the process of removing a hydrogen atom from an alkane, resulting in the formation of an alkene.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a branched alkane?
questions.explanation: Isobutane is an example of a branched alkane due to its three-carbon chain with a methyl group attached to the central carbon.

questions.q: What is the general formula for alkenes?
questions.explanation: Alkenes are unsaturated hydrocarbons with the general formula CnH2n-2. This is because each carbon atom with a double bond has only three bonds.

questions.q: Which of the following alkenes is not an isomer of the other?
questions.explanation: Propene and 2-methylpropene are structural isomers, but 2-methylpropene is also an alkene with a methyl group attached to the double bond, making it different from propene.

questions.q: What is the process of adding a hydrogen atom to an alkene called?
questions.explanation: Hydrogenation is the process of adding a hydrogen atom to an alkene, resulting in the formation of an alkane.

questions.q: Which type of isomerism is exhibited by but-1-ene?
questions.explanation: But-1-ene exhibits structural isomerism due to the presence of different structures, such as 1-butene and 2-methylpropene.

questions.q: What is the general formula for alkynes?
questions.explanation: Alkynes are unsaturated hydrocarbons with the general formula CnH2n-2. This is because each carbon atom with a triple bond has only two bonds.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a terminal alkyne?
questions.explanation: Ethyne is an example of a terminal alkyne, as it has a triple bond at the end of the carbon chain.

questions.q: What is the process of removing a hydrogen atom from an alkyne called?
questions.explanation: Dehydrogenation is the process of removing a hydrogen atom from an alkyne, resulting in the formation of an allene.

questions.q: Which type of isomerism is exhibited by pent-2-yne?
questions.explanation: Pent-2-yne exhibits structural isomerism due to the presence of different structures, such as 1-pentyne and 2-methylbutyne.

questions.q: What is the general formula for aromatic hydrocarbons?
questions.explanation: Aromatic hydrocarbons have the general formula CnH2n+6, which is a result of the delocalization of electrons in the ring.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a benzenoid aromatic hydrocarbon?
questions.explanation: Benzene is an example of a benzenoid aromatic hydrocarbon, as it has a single ring of six carbon atoms.

questions.q: What is the process of adding a hydrogen atom to an aromatic hydrocarbon called?
questions.explanation: Hydrogenation is the process of adding a hydrogen atom to an aromatic hydrocarbon, resulting in the formation of an alkane.

questions.q: Which type of isomerism is exhibited by naphthalene?
questions.explanation: Naphthalene exhibits structural isomerism due to the presence of different structures, such as 1-naphthalene and 2-naphthalene.
questions.topic: Types of Isomerism in Aromatic Hydrocarbons

questions.q: What is the general formula for cycloalkanes?
questions.explanation: Cycloalkanes have the general formula CnH2n, as each carbon atom in the ring has two bonds.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a cycloalkane?
questions.explanation: Cyclohexane is an example of a cycloalkane, as it has a ring of six carbon atoms.

questions.q: What is the process of removing a hydrogen atom from a cycloalkane called?
questions.explanation: Dehydrogenation is the process of removing a hydrogen atom from a cycloalkane, resulting in the formation of a cycloalkene.

questions.q: Which type of isomerism is exhibited by cyclohexane?
questions.explanation: Cyclohexane exhibits structural isomerism due to the presence of different structures, such as chair and boat conformations.

questions.q: What is the general formula for alkyl halides?
questions.explanation: Alkyl halides have the general formula CnXn+1H2n-2X, as each carbon atom has a halogen atom attached to it.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a primary alkyl halide?
questions.explanation: CH3Cl is an example of a primary alkyl halide, as it has only one carbon atom attached to the halogen atom.

questions.q: What is the process of replacing a hydrogen atom in an alkane with a halogen atom called?
questions.explanation: Halogenation is the process of replacing a hydrogen atom in an alkane with a halogen atom, resulting in the formation of an alkyl halide.
